example_prompt: Tokenize a card payment method for an existing Flutterwave v4 customer using encrypted field values from the Flutterwave SDK.

input_schema.description: Body is a discriminated union on `type`. Exactly one of {card, bank_account, mobile_money, opay, applepay, googlepay, ussd} sub-object must be provided to match the chosen type.

type.description: Discriminator selecting which payment instrument to register.

customer_id.description: Identifier of the Flutterwave customer that will own this payment method.

meta.description: Arbitrary string key-value metadata stored alongside the payment method.

card.description: Required when type='card'. All encrypted_* fields use Flutterwave field-level encryption.

nonce.description: 12-character alphanumeric nonce used for field-level encryption.

bank_account.description: Required when type='bank_account'. Currently reserved for future expansion — pass an empty object.

network.description: Mobile money network operator code, e.g. 'MTN', 'AIRTEL', 'VODAFONE'.

country_code.description: Country dialling code as digits only, e.g. '234' for Nigeria.

phone_number.description: Subscriber number digits only, without the country code.

use_qr.description: Set true to request QR-code initiation flow when supported by the network.

opay.description: Required when type='opay'. Currently reserved for future expansion — pass an empty object.

card_holder_name.description: Letters, spaces, commas, periods, apostrophes, and hyphens only.
Letters, spaces, commas, periods, apostrophes, and hyphens only.

account_bank.description: Nigerian bank code, digits only (3 or more), e.g. '050'.

trace_id.description: Optional X-Trace-Id header value for request correlation.

idempotency_key.description: Optional X-Idempotency-Key header to safely retry the same payment method creation.

gotchas: Flutterwave v4 uses OAuth2 client credentials. POST client_id and client_secret as form-encoded body to https://idp.flutterwave.com/realms/flutterwave/protocol/openid-connect/token to obtain an access_token (valid 10 minutes), then use it as Bearer in the Authorization header. The FLUTTERWAVE_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS env var stores base64(client_id:client_secret).

The body is a discriminated union on `type`. For each type the per-type sub-object key matches the type name (card, mobile_money, ussd, applepay, googlepay, bank_account, opay) — there is NO generic `details` field.

Card fields are field-level encrypted: send nonce (12 alphanumeric chars) plus encrypted_card_number / encrypted_expiry_month / encrypted_expiry_year (and optional encrypted_cvv). Generate these using the Flutterwave-provided SDK — never POST raw PAN/CVV directly.
mobile_money requires three fields: network (e.g. 'MTN'), country_code (digits only, 1-3 chars, e.g. '234'), phone_number (digits only, 7-10 chars, without country code).
ussd.account_bank is the Nigerian bank code (digits only, 3+ chars, e.g. '050').
applepay.card_holder_name / googlepay.card_holder_name accept 2-50 chars of letters, spaces, commas, periods, apostrophes and hyphens only.
bank_account and opay sub-objects are documented as reserved for future expansion — pass {} when registering those types.
Send X-Idempotency-Key (12-255 chars) when retrying creation to avoid duplicate stored instruments.
Successful response is 201 Created with the same per-type key echoed back plus device_fingerprint, client_ip, and created_datetime.
